findings.evidence: Line 51 has `noncomputable def conj (h : Y ≃ₜ Y) : AmbientIsotopy Y`; lines 52 and 55-56 already use only `h`, `h.symm`, and `Homeomorph.prodCongr`, whose Mathlib signature is heterogeneous (`prodCongr (h₁ : X ≃ₜ X') (h₂ : Y ≃ₜ Y')`, Mathlib/Topology/Constructions/SumProd.lean:629).
findings.fix: Prove the heterogeneous version first, e.g. with `{Z : Type*} [TopologicalSpace Z] (h : Y ≃ₜ Z) : AmbientIsotopy Z`, and derive the current self-homeomorphism conjugation and endpoint formulas as special cases.
findings.issue: `conj` requires `h : Y ≃ₜ Y`, but the formula only uses a homeomorphism from the source ambient space to the target ambient space; this loses the natural transport API for coordinate changes between homeomorphic spaces.

summary: The construction is stated one level too specifically: the same coordinate-change argument transports an ambient isotopy across any homeomorphism between ambient spaces, with the self-homeomorphism conjugation as a specialization.
